acquisition method: The Q-Exactive was operated in a data-dependent acquisition (DDA) mode
alkylation reagent: with 20 mM iodoacetamide for 30 min for alkylation
alkylation concentration: with 20 mM iodoacetamide for 30 min for alkylation
cleavage agent: using known Kelch domain structures, we mapped the 6 Kelch repeats in KBTBD4 and constructed 3D structural models for KBTBD4WT and oncogenic mutants
collision energy: fragmented on the HCD cell (normalised collision energy of 28)
instrument: using a Dionex Ultimate 3000 UPLC coupled to a Q-Exactive mass spectrometer
